doc_type: Stipulation and [Proposed] Order Extending Time to Respond to Complaint
key_facts: Stipulation is filed exclusively in the federal action (Case No. 2:26-cv-00004-DJC-JDP(PS), U.S. District Court, Eastern District of California); it does not address or extend any deadline in the parallel state action (STK-CV-UF-2026-0001032, San Joaquin Superior Court).

Only defendant Katie Palacios (Director, HOPE Program, Stockton, CA, sued in her individual capacity) is party to the stipulation; co-defendants Agent A. Derrick #6910 and Agent G. Noguchi #7284 (both CDCR parole agents, sued in their individual capacities) are not parties and are not covered by this extension.
Palacios's deadline to move, answer, or otherwise respond to the complaint is extended to April 6, 2026.
Plaintiff Brendan Ngehsi Newanforbi is proceeding pro se and signed the stipulation personally (s/ Brendan Ngehsi Newanforbi, dated April 2, 2026).
Defense counsel of record for Palacios is the firm Sims, Lawrence & Broghammer (Cynthia G. Lawrence, SBN 148927, and Bryan L. Malone, SBN 144082), located in Roseville, CA.
The stipulation expressly preserves all of Palacios's defenses; the extension is not a waiver of any defense.
This is the first and only extension request filed in connection with this federal matter as of the filing date.
The proposed order is addressed to Magistrate Judge Jeremy D. Peterson, the assigned magistrate judge.
The complaint was originally filed January 5, 2026; no trial date has been set (Trial Date: TBD).
The document was filed as Docket Entry 22 in the federal case on April 2, 2026.

dates.event: Stipulation signed by both parties and filed (ECF Doc. 22)
Extended deadline for Katie Palacios to move, answer, or otherwise respond to the complaint

arguments: The parties agree that Palacios needs additional time to respond to the complaint and jointly stipulate to the April 6, 2026 deadline.
No prejudice argument is made against plaintiff; because Newanforbi is pro se and co-signed, the extension is by mutual consent.

Palacios expressly reserves all defenses (procedural, jurisdictional, and substantive), signaling that a motion to dismiss or other responsive pleading — rather than a straightforward answer — is anticipated.
Counsel represent there has been no prior extension request, satisfying any local rule or court preference for a first-request showing.
evidence_cited: Federal complaint filed January 5, 2026 (the operative pleading triggering the response deadline).
Signatures of Cynthia G. Lawrence (on behalf of Sims, Lawrence & Broghammer) and Brendan Ngehsi Newanforbi (pro se plaintiff), both dated April 2, 2026, as mutual agreement of the parties.
Representation that no previous extension has been granted or requested in this federal matter.
procedural_significance: This stipulation establishes that, as of April 2, 2026 (two days before today's date of April 4, 2026), defendant Katie Palacios had secured only a brief two-day extension — to April 6, 2026 — to file her responsive pleading in the federal action. The imminent deadline means a Rule 12 motion or answer from Palacios is expected imminently and should be monitored closely. Critically, the stipulation is federal-only: the parallel state action (STK-CV-UF-2026-0001032) is entirely unaffected, and any response deadlines there remain governed by California Code of Civil Procedure rules independently. The non-participating co-defendants (Derrick and Noguchi, CDCR parole agents) retain their own, separate response obligations in the federal case. The express reservation of all defenses strongly indicates Palacios's counsel is evaluating a motion to dismiss — potentially on qualified immunity, lack of personal liability as a program director, or insufficiency of the pleading — rather than a simple answer. For Matter 5 strategy, plaintiff Newanforbi (pro se) should be prepared for a dispositive motion filing on or shortly after April 6, 2026 in the federal forum, while simultaneously tracking any outstanding state-court deadlines independently.
